title_en: The Intercession, the Pool (Hawd), and the Scales
content_en:
Among the distinctive beliefs of Ahl al-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah are the affirmations of specific eschatological realities that some theological schools either denied or reinterpreted. Imam at-Tahawi affirms the intercession (shafa'ah), the Pool (hawd), and the Scales (mizan) as real, physical realities that will occur in the next life, established by the Quran and the mutawatir Sunnah.

The greatest intercession is that of the Prophet Muhammad, may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him, on the Day of Judgment. This is the 'praised station' (maqam mahmud) mentioned in the Quran: 'And it may be that your Lord will raise you to a praised station' (al-Isra: 79). On that day, when all of humanity is in desperate need, the prophets will each decline the request to intercede with Allah to begin the Judgment, until the matter is brought to Muhammad, may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him, who will prostrate before Allah and then be given permission to intercede. This hadith is reported in both Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim and is among the most well-known narrations in all of Islam.

Intercession, however, is only effective by Allah's permission and only for those whom Allah is pleased with — it is not intercession that bypasses Allah's will or operates independently of His decree. Allah says: 'Who is it that can intercede with Him except by His permission?' (al-Baqarah: 255). This is a critical distinction that separates the Islamic understanding of intercession from pagan or polytheistic concepts in which intermediaries operate independently of a higher divine authority.

The Pool (hawd) is a large body of water given to the Prophet on the Day of Judgment, from which he will offer drink to his followers before they enter Paradise. Its water is whiter than milk, sweeter than honey, and cooler than ice. Drinking from it quenches thirst forever. The hadith describing the hawd reach the level of mutawatir — narrated by so many Companions through so many chains that their collective authenticity is beyond doubt. Imam at-Tahawi affirms this as an article of faith, and Ibn Abi al-Izz notes that denying what has been established by mutawatir narration is a serious theological error.

The Scales (mizan) are real scales on which deeds will be weighed on the Day of Judgment. The Quran states: 'We shall set up scales of justice for the Day of Judgment, so that not a soul will be dealt with unjustly in the least' (al-Anbiya: 47). The weighing of deeds is one of the most frequently mentioned eschatological events in the Quran and Sunnah. The Mu'tazilah interpreted these references metaphorically as referring simply to justice, but Ahl al-Sunnah affirm actual scales befitting the majesty of that Day — scales unlike anything in the material world, yet real in the fullest sense.

These beliefs are not peripheral curiosities but are central to the Muslim's orientation toward the Hereafter. Affirming the intercession cultivates hope in Allah's mercy. Affirming the hawd deepens love for the Prophet. Affirming the mizan reinforces accountability and the urgency of righteous deeds. The Tahawiyyah, by including these among its articles of creed, reminds believers that faith in the unseen realities of the next life is inseparable from faith in Allah and His Messenger.
content_id:
Di antara keyakinan khas ahlus sunnah wal jamaah adalah penegasan terhadap realitas-realitas eskatologis tertentu yang oleh sebagian mazhab teologis diingkari atau ditafsirkan ulang. Imam Al-Tahawi menegaskan syafaat (shafa'ah), telaga (hawd), dan timbangan (mizan) sebagai realitas nyata yang akan terjadi di akhirat, ditetapkan oleh Al-Quran dan Sunnah yang mutawatir.

Syafaat yang paling agung adalah syafaat Nabi Muhammad ﷺ pada hari Kiamat. Inilah 'maqam mahmud' (kedudukan yang terpuji) yang disebutkan dalam Al-Quran: 'Dan semoga Tuhanmu mengangkatmu ke kedudukan yang terpuji' (Al-Isra: 79). Pada hari itu, ketika seluruh umat manusia berada dalam kebutuhan yang sangat mendesak, para nabi satu per satu menolak permintaan untuk bersyafaat kepada Allah agar Pengadilan dimulai, hingga urusan itu sampai kepada Muhammad ﷺ, yang kemudian bersujud di hadapan Allah dan diberi izin untuk bersyafaat. Hadis ini diriwayatkan baik dalam Sahih Al-Bukhari maupun Sahih Muslim dan merupakan salah satu riwayat paling terkenal dalam seluruh khazanah Islam.

Syafaat, bagaimanapun, hanya efektif dengan izin Allah dan hanya untuk orang-orang yang Allah ridhai — ia bukan syafaat yang melampaui kehendak Allah atau beroperasi secara independen dari ketetapan-Nya. Allah berfirman: 'Siapakah yang dapat memberi syafaat di sisi-Nya kecuali dengan izin-Nya?' (Al-Baqarah: 255). Ini adalah pembedaan penting yang memisahkan pemahaman Islam tentang syafaat dari konsep-konsep pagan atau politeistik di mana perantara-perantara beroperasi secara independen dari otoritas Ilahi yang lebih tinggi.

Telaga (hawd) adalah sumber air besar yang diberikan kepada Nabi ﷺ pada hari Kiamat, dari mana beliau akan memberi minum kepada para pengikutnya sebelum mereka memasuki surga. Airnya lebih putih dari susu, lebih manis dari madu, dan lebih dingin dari es. Meminumnya menghilangkan dahaga selamanya. Hadis-hadis yang menggambarkan hawd telah mencapai derajat mutawatir — diriwayatkan oleh begitu banyak sahabat melalui begitu banyak jalur sehingga keasliannya secara kolektif tidak diragukan. Imam Al-Tahawi menegaskan ini sebagai bagian dari akidah, dan Ibn Abi Al-Izz mencatat bahwa mengingkari apa yang telah ditetapkan oleh riwayat mutawatir adalah kesalahan teologis yang serius.

Timbangan (mizan) adalah timbangan nyata yang di atasnya amal perbuatan akan ditimbang pada hari Kiamat. Al-Quran menyatakan: 'Dan Kami menetapkan timbangan-timbangan yang adil untuk hari Kiamat, sehingga tidak ada jiwa yang dirugikan sedikit pun' (Al-Anbiya: 47). Penimbangan amal adalah salah satu peristiwa eskatologis yang paling sering disebutkan dalam Al-Quran dan Sunnah. Mu'tazilah menafsirkan referensi-referensi ini secara metaforis sebagai sekadar mengacu pada keadilan, namun ahlus sunnah wal jamaah menegaskan timbangan yang sesungguhnya yang layak dengan keagungan hari itu — timbangan yang tidak menyerupai apa pun di dunia materi, namun nyata dalam pengertian yang paling penuh.

Keyakinan-keyakinan ini bukan hal-hal pinggiran yang hanya menarik secara intelektual, melainkan merupakan inti dari orientasi seorang Muslim terhadap akhirat. Menegaskan syafaat menumbuhkan harapan pada rahmat Allah. Menegaskan hawd memperdalami kecintaan kepada Nabi ﷺ. Menegaskan mizan memperkuat rasa tanggung jawab dan urgensi amal saleh. Al-Tahawiyyah, dengan memasukkan hal-hal ini ke dalam artikel-artikel akidahnya, mengingatkan kaum mukmin bahwa iman kepada realitas-realitas gaib kehidupan akhirat tidak terpisahkan dari iman kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya.
content_ar: Among the distinctive beliefs of Ahl al-السنة wal-Jama'ah are the affirmations of specific eschatological realities that some theological schools either denied or reinterpreted. الإمام at-Tahawi affirms the intercession (shafa'ah), the Pool (hawd), and the Scales (الميزان) as real, physical realities that will occur in the next الحياة, established by the القرآن الكريم and the mutawatir السنة. The greatest intercession is that of النبي ﷺ محمد ﷺ, may الله's peace and البركات be upon him, on the يوم القيامة. This is the 'praised station' (maqam mahmud) mentioned in the القرآن الكريم: 'And it may be that your Lord will raise you to a praised station' (al-Isra: 79). On that day, عندما all of humanity is in desperate need, the الأنبياء will each decline the request to intercede with الله to begin the Judgment, حتى the matter is brought to محمد ﷺ, may الله's peace and البركات be upon him, who will prostrate قبل الله and then be given permission to intercede. This الحديث is reported in both صحيح البخاري and صحيح مسلم and is among the most well-known narrations in all of الإسلام. Intercession, غير أن, is only effective by الله's permission and only for those whom الله is pleased with — it is not intercession that bypasses الله's will or operates independently of His decree. الله says: 'Who is it that can intercede with Him except by His permission?' (al-Baqarah: 255). This is a البالغ الأهمية distinction that separates the Islamic الفهم of intercession from pagan or polytheistic concepts in which intermediaries operate independently of a higher divine authority. The Pool (hawd) is a large الجسد of water given to النبي ﷺ on the يوم القيامة, from which he will offer drink to his followers قبل they enter الجنة. Its water is whiter than milk, sweeter than honey, and cooler than ice. Drinking from it quenches thirst forever. The الحديث describing the hawd reach the level of mutawatir — narrated by لذا many الصحابة through لذا many chains that their collective authenticity is beyond doubt. الإمام at-Tahawi affirms this as an article of الإيمان, and Ibn Abi al-Izz notes that denying what has been established by mutawatir narration is a serious theological error. The Scales (الميزان) are real scales on which deeds will be weighed on the يوم القيامة. The القرآن الكريم states: 'We shall set up scales of العدل for the يوم القيامة, لذا that not a النفس will be dealt with unjustly in the least' (al-Anbiya: 47). The weighing of deeds is one of the most frequently mentioned eschatological events in the القرآن الكريم and السنة. The المعتزلة interpreted these references metaphorically as referring simply to العدل, but Ahl al-السنة affirm actual scales befitting the majesty of that Day — scales unlike anything in the material الدنيا, yet real in the fullest sense. These beliefs are not peripheral curiosities but are المحوري to the مسلم's orientation toward الآخرة. Affirming the intercession cultivates الأمل
